trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals, watches, and machinery. Taiwan exports semiconductors, electronics, and precision instruments. Taiwan's TSMC and other chip firms are critical suppliers to Swiss tech industry. An EFTA-Taiwan trade agreement is under discussion.

diplomatic.summary: Switzerland does not maintain formal diplomatic relations with Taiwan due to its One China policy, but maintains robust unofficial contacts through the Swiss-Taiwan trade office. Economic and cultural ties are strong. Switzerland supports Taiwan's participation in international organizations on a functional basis (WHO, ICAO).

military.summary: No formal military relations. Switzerland's neutrality precludes any security commitments to Taiwan. Switzerland has not taken explicit positions on Taiwan Strait security.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Swiss pharmaceutical and chemical companies have Taiwan operations. Taiwanese tech companies invest in Swiss precision engineering. Swiss banks manage Taiwanese corporate and private wealth.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Some Swiss investment in Taiwan's renewable energy transition. Taiwan exports clean tech components used in Swiss energy systems.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in semiconductor physics, materials science, and biotech. Swiss and Taiwanese universities maintain exchange programs. Joint publications in engineering and computer science increasing.
